Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Fine mapping plus overexpression and knockout clearly show Cr3a(650) confers tomato fruit cracking resistance; knockout causes cracking and introduction alleviates cracking.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly described: fine mapping, qRT-PCR, transgenic introduction, CRISPR/Cas9 knockout, WGS off-target screening, phenotype observation, and transcriptome-metabolome integration.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper repeatedly analyzes mature green, breaker, and red ripe stages.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Promoter cis-element analysis of native promoters is described, including TGACG, CCAAT, MBS, TGA, ABRE, LTR, and TCA elements in Solyc03g115660.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point matches Results/Conclusion: fine-mapped interval, Cr3a(650) as validated causal gene, knockout/introduction phenotypes, and multi-omics links to four pathways.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The summary and conclusion state promise for molecular breeding and marker-assisted improvement of fruit crack resistance; transgenic/genome editing utility is reasonably supported by performed methods.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper primarily studies fruit cracking resistance, not nutritional quality, and explicitly states Cr3a(660) was tested but excluded because phenotypes did not change as expected.
